name: Target-Company Job-Driven Resume Tailoring Playbook
purpose: A public, abstracted playbook for turning a candidate profile, target company, target role, and JD into a tailored resume and optimization report.
sourcePolicy: This playbook contains abstracted rules and synthetic examples only. It intentionally avoids original third-party guide prose, raw sample resume text, and personal data.

workflow.objective: Normalize the target company, role family, country/market, seniority, JD, and user constraints.
Extract skill, responsibility, domain, tool, stakeholder, metric, and behavioral signals from the JD.
Map user facts to JD signals without inventing facts.
Choose resume sections, ordering, density, and page size for the target market and role.
Rewrite bullets as action, object, method/tool, scope, and outcome.
Explain the changes, gaps, and next evidence needed before final submission.

checks: Must-have skills are proven by experience
Top responsibilities appear in the first half
Tone fits the company and market
Bullets contain action, method, scope, and result
Metrics are used only when supported

evidenceMappingRules: Direct evidence beats keyword matching. A keyword should appear only when a real experience proves it.
When a JD requirement is missing, ask for project, coursework, tool, stakeholder, scale, or result evidence before rewriting.
Weakly related experience should be compressed, not deleted, when it still shows transferable skills.
The first half of the resume should carry the strongest role-specific evidence.
Do not fabricate metrics, company names, tools, awards, dates, or outcomes.

questions: What scale can you safely state: users, rows, revenue, time saved, accuracy, latency, conversion, cost, or team size?
Was there a before/after result, even if approximate?
Which stack, model, API, database, framework, or infrastructure did you personally use?
What part of the system was your responsibility?
Who used the output and what decision or workflow did it affect?
What business or operational problem did this project solve?
Which target company values or job requirements does this experience best support?
Should this experience be framed for technical depth, business impact, leadership, or domain knowledge?

syntheticRewritePatterns.weak: Worked on a data dashboard for the team.
syntheticRewritePatterns.strong: Built a SQL-based KPI dashboard that consolidated weekly sales data and helped managers identify underperforming regions.
syntheticRewritePatterns.why: Adds tool, object, cadence, stakeholder, and decision value without inventing unsupported metrics.

syntheticRewritePatterns.weak: Participated in a machine learning project.
syntheticRewritePatterns.strong: Trained and evaluated a classification model on course project data, comparing baseline and tuned results to explain feature impact.
syntheticRewritePatterns.why: Names model work and evaluation behavior while staying conservative when dataset and metric details are missing.

syntheticRewritePatterns.strong: Synthesized competitor pricing and customer segment findings into a recommendation memo for a student consulting project.
syntheticRewritePatterns.why: Turns vague assistance into analysis output, context, and deliverable.
